cards.back: A technique used by operating systems to reduce the time spent on writing data to disk, by allowing multiple readers to access the same data without having to copy it
Copy-on-write is typically used for files that are not frequently modified, such as read-only files or those that are infrequently accessed

cards.front: What is the purpose of copy-on-write?
cards.back: The primary purpose of copy-on-write is to minimize the number of disk write operations, which can improve system performance
When a writer tries to write to a file that has a copy-on-write attribute, a copy of the data is made on the write operation, allowing multiple readers to access the same data without interference

cards.front: What is the advantage of using copy-on-write?
cards.back: Using copy-on-write can improve system performance by reducing the number of disk write operations, which can improve responsiveness and overall system throughput
